description_long: This is a large, hand-crafted ceramic vase with a bulbous lower body and a slightly flared, narrow neck. The surface is matte and unglazed, showing a natural, earthy beige to light brown coloration with subtle variations and some darker patches. Around the widest part of the vase, there is a faint, dotted ridge that encircles the body, adding a tactile and visual accent. The vase appears to have two small, rounded protrusions on opposite sides, possibly functioning as rudimentary handles or decorative nubs. The overall form is asymmetrical and organic, suggesting it may be of traditional or ancient origin. The surface shows signs of age and wear, with some discoloration and minor surface imperfections.
description: A large, matte ceramic vase with a bulbous body, narrow neck, and subtle dotted ridge around its widest part. The surface is earthy beige with visible wear and small protrusions on the sides.
description_view_0: This view highlights the vase's bulbous body, narrow neck, and a prominent side protrusion with a visible dotted ridge.
description_view_1: This angle shows both side protrusions and the full encircling dotted ridge, with the neck appearing slightly more upright.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, but the opposite side is visible, showing another protrusion and the continuation of the dotted ridge.
description_view_3: This view centers the vase, showing both side protrusions symmetrically and the full width of the body.
